commit.message:
fix: address all adversarial review findings

- utils.py: rename get_project_root() -> get_app_data_dir(), returns
  Path.home() / '.seakarr' so pip-installed paths work correctly

- search.py: return list[dict] | None; None on API failure (transient
  error), [] on successful-but-empty results so callers can distinguish

- filter.py: remove premature filter_by_search_type from build_candidates
  pipeline (ran before disc merging, incorrectly eliminated multi-disc
  albums); pick_best_directory now validates all merged disc groups and
  best_single against _valid_for_type; fallback returns None if nothing
  valid

- scanner.py: skip disc subdirs (CD1/CD2) in main scan loop to avoid
  returning (Album, CD1) as artist/album; combine direct_audio + disc_audio
  for accurate track count; OSError guards on iterdir(); use
  contextlib.suppress; _album_info_from_directory searches disc subdirs
  for tag files

- organizer.py: pre-detect duplicate basenames (two discs can both have
  track01.flac) and use parent/filename when duplicates exist; cleanup is
  deepest-first and includes parent dirs of disc subdirs

- download.py: fix cancel race (only append to cancelled after confirmed
  cancel); fix untracked early-return (cancel + cleanup before returning
  when not active); fix polling hang (remove continue from except, init
  current_by_id before try); move speed_checked=True inside 'if speeds:'
  so re-check is not skipped when no in-progress transfers found yet;
  cancel untracked failed filenames at normal exit

- database.py: add add_download_batch() for atomic batch insert

- cli.py: update import to get_app_data_dir; gate organize_files call
  inside res.success check; validate len(moved) before recording to DB;
  use add_download_batch for atomic recording; guard is_downloaded and
  is_skipped with 'album is not None'; handle None from network_search
  and user_browse (API error -> skip without penalising DB); distinguish
  API error (None) from empty results ([]) in both search paths

fix(ci): resolve all release-pipeline warnings and macOS x86_64 linker failure (#42)

* fix(ci): resolve all release-pipeline warnings and macOS x86_64 linker failure

- filter.rs: wrap unsafe ops in score_vectors_avx2 in explicit unsafe {}
  block (Rust 2024 edition: unsafe fn no longer implicitly creates an
  unsafe context); hsum_pd body is clean (pure register intrinsics, no
  raw pointer ops, so no wrapper needed)
- filter.rs: add 12 unit tests for score_vectors_with_metric covering
  all three metrics (IP/Cosine/L2) at short (scalar path) and long
  (AVX2 path + scalar tail) dimensions, including zero-vector edge cases
- release.yml: fix macOS x86_64 linker error by routing x86_64 matrix
  rows to macos-13 (Intel runner, installs x86_64 Python) and aarch64
  rows to macos-latest (ARM64); add fail-fast: false to macOS strategy
  so one failure no longer cascades and cancels all other macOS jobs
- release.yml: add FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true env to
  suppress Node.js 20 deprecation warnings from checkout/setup-python/
  upload-artifact/download-artifact actions
- ci.yml: add same FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24 env; add
  cargo check -q with RUSTFLAGS=-D warnings step to catch future
  E0133-style warnings as errors in every CI run
- Cargo.toml: add description, repository, homepage to suppress
  'manifest has no description' warning from cargo package (sdist job)

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)
- Encrypted OAuth secrets in system_config to fail Unprotect()
- Users prompted to re-run the GitHub OAuth setup wizard

Now explicitly persists keys to ~/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys,
which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

Also recovered the old key (99f8aade) from the stale
the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

1. Agent messages appeared with doubled content in Discord. The
   PluginObserverBridgeService message buffer accumulated V3 streaming
   deltas AND the final assistant.message (which contains the complete
   text), concatenating them into doubled output. Fixed by adding an
   IsCompleteMessage flag to AgentMessageChunkEvent — set true only for
   V3 assistant.message events. The buffer now skips the final event's
   text when deltas were already buffered. Buffer key changed from
   sessionId to (sessionId, agentId) for multi-agent isolation.

2. Messages sent from Discord never appeared in the web chat UI. The
   SignalR handler unconditionally ignored all UserMessage events,
   assuming they were added optimistically by the web UI. External
   messages (Discord) bypassed the web UI and were lost. Fixed with a
   counted-multiset dedup: ChatView.trackSentMessage() after successful
   API delivery, SignalR handler consumeSentMessage() to distinguish
   optimistic echoes from external messages.

and cursor-based pagination.

Backend:

- SqliteMessageRepository using EnsureSchema pattern (WAL mode)
- BroadcastingSessionEventSink projects UserInputEvent and

Frontend:

- Same fallback on reconnection gap-fill

* fix: use authoritative V3 complete text to prevent cross-turn Discord message duplication

The PluginObserverBridgeService message buffer had a race condition caused
by BroadcastingSessionEventSink's fire-and-forget dispatch pattern. When
the assistant.message (complete text) event won the thread pool race and
processed before all deltas, orphaned deltas accumulated in the buffer and
contaminated the next turn's output — producing doubled/corrupt messages.

Fix: when IsCompleteMessage is true, always use the event's authoritative
complete text and discard buffered deltas. This matches what
BroadcastingSessionEventSink already does for message persistence.

Added regression test simulating the race: final processed before deltas,
orphaned deltas present when next turn arrives.

commit.message:
fix: code review remediation plan (phases 1–6) (#17)

* fix: phase 1 — immediate fixes from code review

- cap inspector notices array at 200 to prevent memory leak in long dev sessions
- add fail_ci_if_error flag to Codecov upload step so coverage regressions block CI
- add console.error fallback when persistence flush fails without custom onError handler
- guard store state() return value to be a plain object with descriptive error
- fix 6 markdown line-length violations causing docs:build failure (MD013)

* fix: phase 2 — correctness fixes from code review

- Fix isStateEqual() circular-reference false positive: visited.has()
  now returns false (conservative) instead of true in both array and
  object branches, so circular refs don't suppress mutation notifications
- Add try-catch + rollback to FSM send(): if the _enter hook throws,
  current is restored to the previous state to keep the FSM consistent
- Extend release.yml tarball validation: assert inspector/index.js and
  persistence/plugin.svelte.js are present; assert no .spec. files leak
- Add .nvmrc (24) to document the recommended Node version for CI parity
- Add engines.node >=20 to package.json to communicate consumer runtime
  requirements

* refactor: phase 3 — type safety improvements from code review

- Add src/lib/internal/marker-helpers.ts with readMarker/writeMarker
  helpers to encapsulate the double-cast pattern for symbol-keyed
  markers, eliminating 4x `as unknown as Record<symbol, X>` usages
- Refactor runtime/store-shell.svelte.ts, runtime/subscriptions.ts, and
  async/plugin.svelte.ts to use readMarker/writeMarker for the
  ASYNC_ACTION_MARKER symbol

* test: phase 4 — additional coverage from code review

- Add src/lib/ssr-and-dispose.spec.ts covering:
  - Manager-level isolation (simulates per-request SSR safety)
  - Plain-object guard error path
  - $dispose stops $subscribe and $onAction notifications
  - $dispose is idempotent (no throw on double-dispose)
  - Plugin onDispose callback fires exactly once
  - Error propagation from synchronous and async actions via $onAction
- Add FSM _enter rollback regression test to fsm.spec.ts: asserts
  current rolls back to the pre-transition state when _enter throws

* docs: phase 5 — documentation improvements from code review

- Document TimeTravelController API: entries, currentIndex, isReplaying,
  goTo() with a member-level table in both plugins.md and types.md
- Clarify pick/omit mutual exclusivity: add note that combining both
  throws at store registration time
- Document TTL + migrate interaction: TTL expiry is evaluated before
  migrate; expired payloads skip migration and fall back to initial state
- Expand sync conflict policy description: explicitly call it
  last-write-wins and note that it is deterministic but not causally
  consistent, with guidance for custom merge strategies
- Expand async concurrency policy descriptions to be more precise about
  each policy's behavior

* ci: phase 6 — CI hardening from code review

- Add 'pnpm audit --prod --audit-level=high' step to ci.yml to catch
  production dependency vulnerabilities on every CI run
- Add 'pnpm exec publint --strict ./dist' step to ci.yml to enforce
  strict publish readiness after every build
- Add version-match assertion to release.yml: block the release job
  when the git tag does not match the version in package.json

* fix: address all four review comments from code review

- ci.yml: add set -o pipefail so tee pipe cannot mask build failures
- ssr-and-dispose.spec.ts: rewrite onDispose test to use createStoreShell
  directly, fixing inaccurate comment and proving exactly-once semantics
  on double-dispose
- docs/reference/types.md: clarify $timeTravel.goTo() mutates live state
  (not truly read-only)
- docs/reference/plugins.md: clarify $timeTravel read-only means
  no new history entries recorded, not no state mutation"

fix: use ideal beat grid instead of aubio's noisy tracker (#22)

aubio's beat tracker produces irregular timestamps that drift up to
4.4s from the ideal grid by beat 29. Replace with a synthesized
evenly-spaced grid: beat[i] = offset + i * (60/BPM), using aubio's
first detected beat as the offset and its tempo detection for BPM.

Also allow gap=1 for medium/hard in level_designer since collision
timing now uses BeatInfo.arrival_time (BPM-independent).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Show total elapsed time from scan start when reconnecting

When reconnecting to a running experiment via polling, the elapsed time
now shows the TOTAL duration from when the scan originally started, not
from when polling started.

Changes:
- /api/scans/<repo> endpoint now returns 'running_experiment_created_at'
  with the directory creation timestamp (when scan started)
- reconnectToRunningExperiment() accepts createdAt parameter
- Elapsed time calculated from actual scan start time using Date objects
- Progress updates show accurate 'elapsed Xm Ys' since start
- Total time on completion is now correct

Benefits:
- Users see realistic progress (e.g., 'elapsed 2m 15s' if scan ran
  that long before browser disconnected)
- Better understanding of total duration vs remaining time
- Accurate timing for 7-minute timeout threshold

Example flow:
- Scan starts at 10:00
- Browser disconnects at 10:02 (2 min elapsed)
- Browser reconnects at 10:05
- UI shows 'elapsed 5m Xs' (total since 10:00)
- Not 'elapsed 3m Xs' (time from reconnect)

exp: EXP-2588 Circadian counter-regulation — night k higher CONFIRMED

H1 CONFIRMED: Night k is systematically higher than day k
  Mean Δk (night-day) = +1.5, median = +0.8
  6/12 night higher, 2/12 day higher, 4/12 similar
  Most dramatic: patients a,d both show day k=1.0, night k=7.0

H2 CONFIRMED: Night corrections have stronger counter-regulation
  Consistent with dawn phenomenon / overnight HGP
  Sim overestimates correction drops more at night

Practical implication: forward sim should use time-specific k:
  Day (06-22): lower k (mean 3.1, median 2.2)
  Night (22-06): higher k (mean 4.5, median 3.8)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

exp: EXP-2587 TIR-based counter-reg k prediction — NOT CONFIRMED

All hypotheses NOT CONFIRMED:
  H1: Best single-feature LOO MAE=1.16 (TIR), barely beats
      population median k=1.5 (MAE=1.21). Not < 1.0 threshold.
  H2: TIR correlation r=-0.539 exists but LOO prediction is unstable.
  H3: Multi-feature models overfit catastrophically (14 patients too few).

Key finding: cannot shortcut correction-based k calibration with glucose
metrics. Population median k=1.5 remains the best fallback when
< MIN_CORRECTIONS available for direct calibration.

Validates the design in settings_advisor: direct correction calibration
is essential, population k=1.5 fallback is optimal for cold start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(migrate/registrar): eliminate dead None-arm branch in register_skips_already_registered

The `if let Some(content) = written` at line 190 of the test created a
branch whose None arm was unreachable: `register_plugins` always writes
the marketplace file even when all entries are already registered, so
`get_written` never returns None.

Replace the guarded `if let` with direct `expect()` calls (permitted in
tests per `clippy.toml`) to eliminate the dead branch and strengthen the
assertion — the test now fails explicitly if the file wasn't written.

Branch coverage: 95.45% → 95.50%

commit.message:
fix: rebuild AnimationStudio with correct timeline layout

- Replace broken sticky cursor (was using position:sticky with left which doesn't work as vertical line)
- Cursor now renders as position:absolute overlay on the outer panel, positioned via scrollLeft tracking — always visible above sticky elements
- Remove non-reactive getState() calls for totalDuration in TrackRow (now passed as prop)
- Fix Ruler seek: accounts for LABEL_W offset correctly
- Simplify layout: single overflow:auto scroll container with minWidth content block
- Ruler row uses position:sticky top:0, track labels use position:sticky left:0 — both work reliably in a single scroll axis container
- Auto-scroll keeps cursor in view during playback
- Clip bar shows correct width based on last keyframe time
- All keyframe lanes use reactive totalDuration prop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Enable SourceLink, deterministic builds, and embedded PDB

- Add Microsoft.SourceLink.GitHub for source link support
- Set DebugType to embedded so symbols ship inside the DLL
- Enable PublishRepositoryUrl and EmbedUntrackedSources
- Enable Deterministic; ContinuousIntegrationBuild on CI only

Resolves NuGet Package Explorer warnings for missing symbols,
non-deterministic build, and missing compiler flags.

commit.message:
Hide section placeholder when reconnecting to running scan

The 'Run or load a scan to view section details' placeholder was still
visible when reconnecting to a running experiment. This section-placeholder
is separate from the log output placeholder.

Now when reconnecting:
- Both the log placeholder and section placeholder are hidden
- Only the reconnect status messages are visible
- The UI clearly shows a scan is in progress

This fixes the confusing UX where multiple placeholders appeared at once.
